action: Service-page CTA + tracking audit → form-deploy recommendation
why: Service pages have a Call Now button (not a form). The reported '0 form-conversions on 24K sessions / 365d' may overstate the gap if tap-to-call events aren't firing as GA4 conversions — some of those visitors likely already convert via phone, just untracked. Audit the tracking state first; size the real loss; THEN recommend the form. Form add is still the right call: even with tap-to-call working, a 4-input form on high-intent service pages typically lifts total conversions by capturing the share of users who won't make a phone call.
how: Step 1: deliver a 1-page recommendation memo to AA's dev/marketing owner — model (24K sessions × 2-3% form rate = +480-720/yr conversions), scope (reuse the same 4-input form already on homepage + /contact + /admissions), dev effort estimate (Webflow component reuse — 1-2 days), tracking plan (GA4 form_submit event firing per URL). Step 2: pull GA4 events via API to verify whether tap-to-call on existing Call Now is firing as a conversion event — if not, that's a tracking-gap fallback to ship while form approval is in flight. Step 3: own AA-side approval chase as a 30-day target with weekly status logged.
acceptance: Memo delivered + GA4 tap-to-call audit complete + dev approval status logged weekly

action: Rewrite homepage og:description + meta description

Internal links FROM homepage + /inpatient/adult-rehabilitation + insurance article batch. Watch cannibalization with /inpatient/adult-rehabilitation — route /alcohol-rehab-new-york to top-of-funnel state-level intent.
